title: salix fulvopubescens var. tagawana
intro: Salix fulvopubescens var. tagawana, also known as the Japanese golden willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Japan. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Salix fulvopubescens var. tagawana include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or decoction, and can also be used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and infusions.
